import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let parts = string.split_whitespace(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < parts.len()
    match parts.get(i)
    when Some(tok)
      match string.parse_int(tok)
      when Some(n)
        result = list.push(result, n)
      when None
        ()
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn read_or_empty() -> String
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = read_or_empty()
  let line2 = read_or_empty()
  let a = parse_line(line1)
  let b = parse_line(line2)
  mut all: List<Int> = a
  mut j = 0
  while j < b.len()
    match b.get(j)
    when Some(x)
      all = list.push(all, x)
    when None
      ()
    end
    j = j + 1
  end

  match list.min(all)
  when None
    println("")
  when Some(start)
    mut output = "#{start}"
    mut current = start
    mut done = false
    while not done
      mut next_val = 0
      mut found = false
      mut k = 0
      while k < all.len()
        match all.get(k)
        when Some(v)
          if v > current
            if not found
              next_val = v
              found = true
            else if v < next_val
              next_val = v
            end
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
        k = k + 1
      end
      if found
        output = "#{output} #{next_val}"
        current = next_val
      else
        done = true
      end
    end
    println(output)
  end
end
